idea-plugin.name: PXF — Proto eXpressive Format
idea-plugin.description:
Syntax highlighting and basic editor support for PXF (Proto eXpressive Format) files in any JetBrains IDE — IntelliJ IDEA, GoLand, PyCharm, RustRover, WebStorm, CLion, RubyMine, PhpStorm, Rider, Android Studio.

The plugin bundles a TextMate grammar and registers it on first project open, so you don't need to use Settings → Editor → TextMate Bundles → Add Bundle manually. It also adds a New → PXF File entry to the project context menu.

See protowire.org for the format specification, the EBNF grammar, and reference implementations across nine languages.
idea-plugin.change-notes:
0.1.1

  • Parser stricter on key forms (matches updated grammar): = and { … } require an identifier key; top-level : (map-entry form) is rejected — use = for top-level field assignments.
  • Identifiers used as block keys (e.g. nested_field { … }) now get the same variable.other.member highlight as keys on the left of = and :.

0.1.0

  • Initial release: TextMate-based syntax highlighting for .pxf files (comments, @type directive, strings, bytes, RFC 3339 timestamps, durations, integers, floats, booleans, null, enum values, field assignments, map entries).
  • Bracket matching, auto-closing pairs, brace folding.
  • Comment toggle (⌘/) for #, //, and /* */.
  • New → PXF File action with a starter template.
  • Inline parse-error squiggles via the bundled protowire-java parser (syntax errors only — schema-aware validation comes in a follow-up release).

action.description: Create a new PXF (Proto eXpressive Format) file
